title: Plan a Stage 3 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 3 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 3 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 3 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Digit” mean?
options: A single symbol from 0 to 9
What a digit is worth from its position
The three places in a three-digit number
A number written as the sum of its parts, like 300 + 20 + 7
questions.answer: A single symbol from 0 to 9
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digit means A single symbol from 0 to 9.

questions.question: What does “Place value” mean?
options: What a digit is worth from its position
The three places in a three-digit number
A number written as the sum of its parts, like 300 + 20 + 7
A zero that keeps a place empty, like the 0 in 504
questions.answer: What a digit is worth from its position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Place value means What a digit is worth from its position.

questions.question: What does “Statistics” mean?
options: Collecting, organising and understanding information
The information you collect
Asking many people the same question
Recording data with marks grouped in fives
questions.answer: Collecting, organising and understanding information
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Statistics means Collecting, organising and understanding information.
questions.unitTitle: Statistics: Tally Charts and Frequency Tables

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: The information you collect
Asking many people the same question
Recording data with marks grouped in fives
How many times something happens
questions.answer: The information you collect
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means The information you collect.
questions.unitTitle: Statistics: Tally Charts and Frequency Tables

questions.question: What does “Sum” mean?
options: The answer to an addition
The answer to a subtraction
Trade ten of one place for one of the next
A rough answer found by rounding first
questions.answer: The answer to an addition
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sum means The answer to an addition.

questions.question: What does “Difference” mean?
options: The answer to a subtraction
Trade ten of one place for one of the next
A rough answer found by rounding first
Change a number to the nearest 10 or 100
questions.answer: The answer to a subtraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Difference means The answer to a subtraction.

questions.question: What does “Face” mean?
options: A surface of a solid; flat or curved
A line where two faces meet
A corner where edges meet
Two identical ends, same cross-section
questions.answer: A surface of a solid; flat or curved
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Face means A surface of a solid; flat or curved.

questions.question: What does “Edge” mean?
options: A line where two faces meet
A corner where edges meet
Two identical ends, same cross-section
One base, faces meet at an apex
questions.answer: A line where two faces meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Edge means A line where two faces meet.

questions.question: What does “Factor” mean?
options: A number that is multiplied
The answer to a multiplication
Equal rows and columns of objects
The amount being divided
questions.answer: A number that is multiplied
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Factor means A number that is multiplied.

questions.question: What does “Product” mean?
options: The answer to a multiplication
Equal rows and columns of objects
The amount being divided
The number you divide by
questions.answer: The answer to a multiplication
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Product means The answer to a multiplication.

questions.question: What does “Length” mean?
options: How long something is, end to end
The distance around a shape
The flat space inside a shape
A standard amount for measuring
questions.answer: How long something is, end to end
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Length means How long something is, end to end.

questions.question: What does “Perimeter” mean?
options: The distance around a shape
The flat space inside a shape
A standard amount for measuring
A square used to measure area (cm², m²)
questions.answer: The distance around a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Perimeter means The distance around a shape.

questions.question: What does “Fraction” mean?
options: An equal part of a whole or a set
Top number – how many parts you have
Bottom number – equal parts in the whole
Parts that are the same size
questions.answer: An equal part of a whole or a set
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Fraction means An equal part of a whole or a set.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: Top number – how many parts you have
Bottom number – equal parts in the whole
Parts that are the same size
1 of 2 / 1 of 3 / 1 of 4 equal parts
questions.answer: Top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means Top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Analogue clock” mean?
options: A round clock with moving hands
A clock showing the time as numbers
Short hand for hours, long hand for minutes
Exact hour / thirty minutes past
questions.answer: A round clock with moving hands
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Analogue clock means A round clock with moving hands.

questions.question: What does “Digital clock” mean?
options: A clock showing the time as numbers
Short hand for hours, long hand for minutes
Exact hour / thirty minutes past
Fifteen minutes past / fifteen before the next hour
questions.answer: A clock showing the time as numbers
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Digital clock means A clock showing the time as numbers.

questions.question: What does “Sum” mean?
options: The answer to an addition
The answer to a subtraction
Carrying or borrowing between places
A number split into hundreds + tens + ones
questions.answer: The answer to an addition
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sum means The answer to an addition.

questions.question: What does “Difference” mean?
options: The answer to a subtraction
Carrying or borrowing between places
A number split into hundreds + tens + ones
A rounded, rough answer used to check
questions.answer: The answer to a subtraction
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Difference means The answer to a subtraction.

questions.question: What does “Data” mean?
options: Information you collect, often numbers
A graph using pictures or symbols
What each symbol in a pictogram is worth
A graph using bars to compare amounts
questions.answer: Information you collect, often numbers
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Data means Information you collect, often numbers.

questions.question: What does “Pictogram” mean?
options: A graph using pictures or symbols
What each symbol in a pictogram is worth
A graph using bars to compare amounts
A labelled line on the side or bottom
questions.answer: A graph using pictures or symbols
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Pictogram means A graph using pictures or symbols.

questions.question: What does “Product” mean?
options: The answer to a multiplication
The answer to a division
The number being divided
The number you divide by
questions.answer: The answer to a multiplication
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Product means The answer to a multiplication.

questions.question: What does “Quotient” mean?
options: The answer to a division
The number being divided
The number you divide by
The amount left over after dividing
questions.answer: The answer to a division
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Quotient means The answer to a division.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts
Different fractions with the same value
Write a fraction in its lowest terms
questions.answer: The top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Denominator” mean?
options: The bottom number – how many equal parts
Different fractions with the same value
Write a fraction in its lowest terms
A known fraction (0, 1/2, 1) used to compare
questions.answer: The bottom number – how many equal parts
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Denominator means The bottom number – how many equal parts.

questions.question: What does “Length” mean?
options: How long, tall or far something is
How heavy something is
How much liquid a container holds
A sensible guess before measuring
questions.answer: How long, tall or far something is
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Length means How long, tall or far something is.

questions.question: What does “Mass” mean?
options: How heavy something is
How much liquid a container holds
A sensible guess before measuring
Change from one unit to another
questions.answer: How heavy something is
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Mass means How heavy something is.

questions.question: What does “a.m. / p.m.” mean?
options: Morning / afternoon-and-night on the 12-hour clock
Uses 00:00 to 23:59, no a.m. or p.m.
How long something lasts (its duration)
questions.answer: Morning / afternoon-and-night on the 12-hour clock
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: a.m. / p.m. means Morning / afternoon-and-night on the 12-hour clock.

questions.question: What does “24-hour clock” mean?
options: Uses 00:00 to 23:59, no a.m. or p.m.
How long something lasts (its duration)
A chart of when events happen
questions.answer: Uses 00:00 to 23:59, no a.m. or p.m.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: 24-hour clock means Uses 00:00 to 23:59, no a.m. or p.m..

questions.question: What does “Angle” mean?
options: The amount of turn between two lines meeting at a point
The point where the two lines of an angle meet
A quarter turn; a square corner; 90°
Smaller than a right angle (less than 90°)
questions.answer: The amount of turn between two lines meeting at a point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Angle means The amount of turn between two lines meeting at a point.

questions.question: What does “Vertex” mean?
options: The point where the two lines of an angle meet
A quarter turn; a square corner; 90°
Smaller than a right angle (less than 90°)
Bigger than a right angle but less than 180°
questions.answer: The point where the two lines of an angle meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Vertex means The point where the two lines of an angle meet.

questions.question: What does “Chance / Probability” mean?
options: How likely something is to happen
Definitely will / definitely will not happen
High chance / low chance
Just as likely to happen as not (one half)
questions.answer: How likely something is to happen
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Chance / Probability means How likely something is to happen.

questions.question: What does “Certain / Impossible” mean?
options: Definitely will / definitely will not happen
High chance / low chance
Just as likely to happen as not (one half)
One possible result of an experiment
questions.answer: Definitely will / definitely will not happen
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Certain / Impossible means Definitely will / definitely will not happen.

questions.question: What does “Pattern” mean?
options: An arrangement that follows a rule
The repeating part of a repeating pattern
How a growing pattern changes each step
One item or number in a pattern
questions.answer: An arrangement that follows a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Pattern means An arrangement that follows a rule.

questions.question: What does “Core” mean?
options: The repeating part of a repeating pattern
How a growing pattern changes each step
One item or number in a pattern
When one part mirrors another part
questions.answer: The repeating part of a repeating pattern
questions.hint: Use the Unit 17 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Core means The repeating part of a repeating pattern.

questions.question: What does “Probability” mean?
options: How likely something is to happen
One single try of the experiment
A possible result of a trial
Marks that count / how many times it happened
questions.answer: How likely something is to happen
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Probability means How likely something is to happen.

questions.question: What does “Trial” mean?
options: One single try of the experiment
A possible result of a trial
Marks that count / how many times it happened
Frequency ÷ total trials
questions.answer: One single try of the experiment
questions.hint: Use the Unit 18 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Trial means One single try of the experiment.
